name: k562 Cell-Type Selective Enhancer Activity

tolerance.note: Constraint oracles may drift at most ±5% from the start-sequence baseline. Matches the released DNA optimization runs.

optimization_oracle.note: 3-layer CNN (128→192→256 channels, global max pool). Trained with seed=42.

evaluation_oracle.note: Larger 4-layer CNN (192→256→320→320, dual max+mean pool). Trained independently with seed=99 and a different train/val/test split to detect reward hacking.

biology_note: DNA enhancer sequences from the STARR-seq assay (Bergman et al. 2022). Each sequence drives transcription in a cell-type-specific manner. k562 is a chronic myelogenous leukemia cell line; hepG2 is a hepatocellular carcinoma line; SK-N-SH is a neuroblastoma line. Selective enhancer activity is clinically relevant for gene therapy payload delivery. The task challenges the optimizer to find mutations that increase k562 activity (MAPK/oncogenic context) while preserving the regulatory grammar for the other two lineages.
dataset_citation: Bergman et al. (2022). Assessing the functionality of genomic regions. Nature Genetics.
